evals.prompt: Implement the NotificationPreferencesPanel component. Stack: Vue/Nuxt with Composition API. The panel displays notification preferences (email, SMS, push) per event type and allows toggling each. State must be managed through a Pinia store, not local component state. A SecurityAlert preference must always be enabled for email — this invariant is enforced in the store, not the component. Produce an implementation-summary contract.
evals.expected_output: Vue component using Composition API with script setup, Pinia store for state, SecurityAlert email toggle disabled in UI with invariant enforced in store.
expectations: Composition API with <script setup lang='ts'>
State managed through Pinia store — not local ref/reactive
SecurityAlert email preference always enabled — invariant in store
Component does not contain business logic (invariant enforcement)
Toggle emits domain-named action to store
TypeScript types for preferences — no any
Loading and error states handled
implementation-summary contract produced with all required fields

evals.prompt: Implement the OrderHistoryList component. Stack: React. The tech-lead provided an API contract from the architect, but the actual API response has different field names (snake_case vs camelCase) and a missing 'currency' field on line items. Implement the component and handle any contract mismatches appropriately.
evals.expected_output: Component implementation that flags the API/ADR contract mismatch to the tech-lead rather than silently mapping fields or working around the missing currency field.
expectations: Identifies the field naming mismatch (snake_case vs camelCase)
Identifies the missing currency field on line items
Flags both issues to tech-lead — does NOT silently adapt or work around
Does not create a mapping layer without authorization
Escalation message is specific: lists exact field discrepancies
May implement what is possible but clearly marks contract gaps
Does not invent a default currency value

evals.prompt: Implement the OrderSummary component. Stack: Leptos (Rust/WASM). The component receives a ReadSignal<Order> prop, displays order items with a computed total via create_memo, and has a Confirm Order button that uses create_server_action. The button should only appear for Draft orders. Handle pending and error states. No unwrap() or panic!() in component code. The component name must use domain language. Produce an implementation-summary contract.
evals.expected_output: Leptos component named OrderSummary with ReadSignal<Order> prop, create_memo for total, create_server_action for confirm, pending/error states, Draft-only button, no unwrap/panic.
expectations: Component prop is ReadSignal<Order> — not owned Order or global state
Total computed via create_memo (pure derived state)
Confirm action via create_server_action — not direct HTTP
Button shows 'Confirming...' or similar when pending, and is disabled
Error displayed from action value — not silently swallowed
Confirm button only visible for Draft orders (Show/match)
No unwrap() in component code
No panic!() in component code
Component named OrderSummary (domain language)
For component with key for item list rendering
implementation-summary contract produced with all required fields
